subject: RE: Re: 3D denoising - output black reconstruction
body:
Hi Dmitry,
 
Thanks for the swift reply – appreciated! I’ll download the new installer and give things ago before updating you. 
 
I’ve not managed to get anything denoised yet unfortunately, I’ll keep plugging away!
 
Thanks for the tips re: learning rate and faster testing.
 
Cheers,
 
Alister
 
Sent from 
Mail
 for Windows 10
 
From: 
Dimitry Tegunov
Sent: 
Friday, January 17, 2020 12:40 AM
To: 
Warp
Subject: 
Re: 3D denoising - output black reconstruction
 
Ouch, 60k iterations! Sorry it didn't lead to a result :-( I can't remember if I ever tested batchsize = 1, but the training gradient might become quite unstable in that case. Have you previously succeeded in denoising anything with that configuration? There is a new version of Noise2Map in this installer: https://www.dropbox.com/s/issib5n0zmtqbc6/Warp_20200114.msi?dl=0. It has a couple of new parameters, but the user guide hasn't caught up yet. The descriptions in the help text might be enough though. Briefly, there are multiple networks of different width included. Maybe try --start_model noisenet3dmodel_64, that's the narrowest one. Train for a few hundred iterations and check if the loss value displayed during training experiences sudden changes or even goes NaN, and if the output from denoising is still black. If this happens early during training, try setting --learningrate_start lower than its default value.
 
Cheers,
Dimitry
On Thursday, January 16, 2020 at 10:20:21 AM UTC+1, Alister Burt wrote:
Hi all,
 
Does anyone have experience with using 3D denoising to denoise tomograms with noise2map? I ran it on one tomogram with the following command
 
noise2map --observation1 even --observation2 odd --dont_flatten_spectrum --iterations 15000 --batchsize 1 --gpuid_network 0 --gpuid_preprocess 0
 
This seemed to work fine, giving the following output...

However the final denoised tomogram is black. I don't think this is a memory issue (enough memory free during both training and denoising), have I made a silly mistake?
 
Best,
 
Alister
